intake.user_request: coderef-pipeline CLI has weak project path UX — requires --project-dir flag but this is undiscoverable (positional path fails silently). User hit this during gridiron-franchise scan+populate run. Scope: audit coderef-pipeline.js CLI flag design, compare --project-dir vs positional arg convention, stub a workorder for fixing the UX gap (better error message, --help output, or positional fallback).

classification.plan_type_rationale: Rule 6 fallthrough: focused CLI UX fix — single file (coderef-pipeline.js), no architectural change, no multi-layer impact. Light plan sufficient.

in_scope: Audit coderef-pipeline.js CLI flag design: --project-dir vs positional arg convention
Add clear error message when --project-dir is omitted (fail with actionable hint, not silent)
Improve --help output to surface --project-dir prominently as the primary required arg
Optionally add positional path fallback so `coderef-pipeline <path>` works without the flag
Verify fix against gridiron-franchise scan+populate use case
out_of_scope: Changes to other CLI commands (coderef-scan, populate-coderef, rag-index, etc.)
Structural refactor of the pipeline orchestration logic
Adding new pipeline stages or flags unrelated to path UX
Test suite changes beyond a basic smoke test for the new error path

constraints: Positional path fallback (if added) must not break existing --project-dir users
Error message must name the exact flag: --project-dir <path>
CODEREF-CORE CLI reference doc (docs/CLI.md) must be updated if --help output changes
